lesson.menu_title: My Everyday Life -- Routines & Free Time
lesson.menu_desc: Describing your routine and free time with adverbs of frequency -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 2 -- My Everyday Life
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari | Lesson 2 -- My Everyday Life

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: a
a quick trip to do a small task, like the bank or the pharmacy
b
a small job you do at home, such as cleaning or the dishes
c
the regular trip between your home and your workplace
d
a period of physical exercise, like running or the gym
e
free time when you rest and do the things you enjoy
f
to relax and let go of stress after a busy day
g
the food and everyday items you buy at the supermarket
h
a short sleep, usually taken during the day
i
a slow, relaxed walk that you take for pleasure
j
something you do regularly, often without thinking about it
k
to reconnect with someone, or finish something you were behind on
l
a plan that shows the times when you do each activity

paras: Everybody needs time to unwind, but people around the world relax in very different ways. Our daily habits often depend on the culture we grow up in. Some people always keep a busy schedule, while others usually make time for leisure. One thing is true everywhere: a little rest is never a waste of time.

In Italy, families often take a slow evening stroll after dinner. They call it the passeggiata, and it is a daily habit in many towns. In parts of Spain, people sometimes take a short nap in the afternoon. This rest, the famous siesta, helps them feel fresh for the evening. In big cities, workers rarely leave the office early, so they catch up on sleep on the train home.

You do not need to travel to build a healthier routine. First, look at your schedule and find small moments to relax. After your chores and errands are done, you can read, walk, or simply breathe. Doctors say we should never skip these little breaks. A short stroll or a quiet cup of tea is usually enough to help you unwind.
reading.source: Alumni by Better -- B1 reading, adapted from a wellness column

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: The text teaches you how to cook traditional meals in Italy and Spain.
People relax in different ways around the world, and small daily habits help us unwind.
The text says that office workers never sleep enough to be healthy.

items: In Italy, many families take a slow stroll after dinner.
The text says: 'families often take a slow evening stroll after dinner.'
The siesta is a short nap taken in the afternoon in parts of Spain.
The text says: 'people sometimes take a short nap in the afternoon... the famous siesta.'
Doctors say you should skip your little breaks.
The text says: 'we should never skip these little breaks.'
You need to travel abroad to build a healthier routine.
The text says: 'You do not need to travel to build a healthier routine.'

parts: drink my coffee first -- every single day. I
go for a workout before breakfast, but not every day. My commute
takes about thirty minutes. During the week I
take an afternoon nap, because I am always busy. In the evening, I
go for a stroll to unwind.

gapfill.text: Adverbs of frequency go BEFORE the main verb (I always drink coffee) but AFTER the verb to be (I am always busy). Time expressions like 'every day' or 'twice a week' usually go at the end.

bank.label: Useful sentences to describe your routine
items: I usually wake up early and go for a workout.
My commute always takes about thirty minutes.
I sometimes take a short nap on the weekend.
After work, I often go for a stroll to unwind.
I rarely skip my morning coffee.
On my day off, I like to catch up with friends.
I do the groceries once a week.
Reading before bed is a good habit for me.

lf.text: Notice: the adverb comes BEFORE the main verb (I always drink) but AFTER the verb to be (She is never late).

items.situation: Tell me one thing you always do in the morning.
tips: The first thing I do is ...

items.situation: How often do you exercise? Answer with an adverb of frequency.
tips: I sometimes go for a run.

items.situation: Name one chore you never enjoy doing.
What do you usually do to unwind after a long day?
Describe your commute. How long does it usually take?

items: Describe your typical morning in three sentences. Use always, usually, and sometimes.
Now talk about your free time: what you often do on the weekend to unwind.
Describe a full day, from morning to evening, with no notes. Use at least four adverbs of frequency.
A friend asks: 'Do you ever take a nap during the day?' Answer honestly with an adverb of frequency.

answerkey.title: Reveal the model description
list: Model: 'I usually wake up early and go for a short workout. My commute always takes about thirty minutes. During the week I rarely have time to relax, but on the weekend I often go for a long stroll to unwind. I sometimes take a nap in the afternoon, and I never skip my morning coffee.'
Before the main verb: I ALWAYS drink coffee. / She USUALLY walks to work.
After the verb to be: He is OFTEN tired. / They are NEVER late.
Time expressions go at the end: I go for a stroll EVERY EVENING.
answerkey.note: Order of frequency: always > usually > often > sometimes > rarely > never. The adverb sits before the main verb, but after am / is / are.

listenings.text: Hello, and welcome to your daily wellness minute. Here is a simple idea: small habits make a big difference. In the morning, try to always drink a glass of water before your coffee. During the day, take short breaks. Even a five minute stroll helps you unwind. If you feel tired after lunch, a quick nap is often better than another coffee. And remember, you rarely need a long vacation to feel better. A calm evening routine usually works just as well. Take care, and see you tomorrow.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah. I hope you are having a calm week. Listen, I usually work on Saturdays, but this weekend I am free. Do you want to catch up? We could take a long stroll in the park, and then I need to do my groceries. I rarely have a full day off, so I really want to relax and enjoy it. On Sunday I always visit my mother, but Saturday is open. Call me back and let me know. Talk soon. Bye!

extra_audio.text: In the morning, I always drink my coffee first. I usually go for a short workout before work. My commute often takes about thirty minutes. In the evening, I sometimes go for a stroll to unwind. On the weekend, I catch up with friends and do the groceries.
